close_reason: Arc<Mutex> serialization is correct for wolfhsm which has a single transport connection. RwLock would be unsafe since all HSM ops are writes.
description: lib.rs:106-107 wraps the wolfhsm::Client in Arc<Mutex<WolfHsmState>> and lib.rs:420 holds the lock for the entire spawn_blocking section. Concurrent get() calls on a single WolfHsmBackend serialize even though wolfHSM operations are independent. wolfhsm::Client is !Sync (single-threaded C state) so the mutex is required for safety, but a clone-friendly design (each Arc<WolfHsmBackend> would own its own Client, or a small client pool) could allow concurrency. For workloads with N readers of the same secret this is currently a bottleneck. Probably fine for typical secret-loading use; flag as scaling concern, not bug.
title: Arc<Mutex<WolfHsmState>> serializes all concurrent reads and writes against the same backend
